rulings.Case Title: ROBERT COURTNIER VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Ductmate Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 6, 2015. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY BUDDLE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication as to the claims of plaintiffs Mary Buddle and Cheryl Ahrens is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Ellory Buddle was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
